title: Research Source-Note — v1
description: One provenance record per cited source in the research knowledge base. A source-note is the atom of trust: a claim may only cite a source-note that exists and validates here. Implements the provenance contract in docs/planning/research/_meta/charter.md and trust-policy.md. Provenance depth (owner decision, 2026-06-01): checksums + provenance JSON only — Cosign/SLSA are deferred opt-ins, so signature fields are reserved but never required.

id.description: Stable source-note identifier. Claims reference this id.

title.description: Human title of the source (document, page, spec, repo, or talk).

source_tier.description: Trust tier per trust-policy.md. authoritative-standard = AU gov / international standards (ACSC ISM, PSPF, NIST, SLSA); official-guidance = official gov or first-party vendor guidance; primary = first-party spec/repo/RFC/commit; practitioner-signal = blogs, conference talks, the YouTube corpus (down-ranked); community = forums, Q&A, social.

publisher.description: Issuing organisation or author (e.g. 'Australian Cyber Security Centre', 'NIST', vendor, channel).

url.description: Canonical URL for external sources. Required unless the source is codebase-internal (then use locator.kind = codebase).

published_date.description: Date the source itself was published/last revised, where knowable. Distinct from retrieval_date.

retrieval_date.description: Date this source was retrieved/accessed. Every source-note carries a retrieval date — no exceptions (citation-without-date is a red flag).

accessed_via.description: How the source was obtained. 'deep-research' = normalised from the deep-research engine; 'codebase' = self-validation against this repo (owner-approved); 'imported-corpus' = ingested under _imported/.

locator.description: Where the cited content lives, precisely enough to re-find it.

kind.description: web = URL; codebase = repo path; document = standard/spec section; corpus = entry in an _imported corpus.

ref.description: The locator value: a URL, a 'path@gitsha' for codebase, a 'standard §section' for document, or a corpus record id.

anchor.description: Optional sub-location: heading, line range, control id, or timestamp.

classification.description: PSPF classification of the source-note's content. Defaults to OFFICIAL; escalate per trust-policy.md classification handling.

trust.description: Origin trust label. First-party authored = trusted; imported/fetched/third-party = untrusted. Untrusted source-notes live under _staging/ or _imported/ and cannot back a canonical claim until reviewed and promoted.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 of the retrieved content (the provenance checksum). Lets a consumer verify what was recorded. Proves what was recorded at retrieval time, NOT downstream behaviour (corpus anti-hype: recorded != executed).

excerpt.description: Verbatim quoted evidence supporting any claim that cites this note. Quote, do not paraphrase.

license.description: Source licence/terms where relevant (e.g. for reuse of standard text).

retrieved_by.description: Agent or person who retrieved the source (e.g. '@researcher', 'deep-research').

signature.description: RESERVED — opt-in cryptographic signature over content_hash (Cosign/SLSA). Deferred per owner decision 2026-06-01; never required at v1.

notes.description: Free-text provenance notes (e.g. retrieval caveats, paywall, archived copy).
